title: Scalar subquery with "LIMIT 0" should return NULL
poc:
end of the following code should be NULL, not 1:
CREATE TABLE t1(x);
key_bug_features: Scalar subquery with LIMIT 0 returns incorrect value
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2(a INTEGER, b TEXT, c REAL, d BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO t2(a, b, c, d) VALUES(1, 'test', 3.14, 1);
SELECT (SELECT a FROM t2 WHERE b = 'nonexistent' LIMIT 0);
INSERT INTO t2(a, b, c, d) VALUES(NULL, 'null_test', NULL, NULL);
SELECT (SELECT c FROM t2 WHERE d IS NULL LIMIT 0);
